Settings Help Legal Sign Out

My Search

Save
  • 100 Results
Result - #1
  • 2 Versions
  • •
  • 1 Attachment
  • •
  • Version ID: #123

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

Product Type: Mortgages Open / Closed: Closed End, Open End Fixed / Adjustable: Adjustable, Fixed Property Type: Condo, Single Family Max # of Properties: Up to 6 Properties, Multi-Family Occupancy: Primary or Secondary, Non-Owner Occupied Loan Purpose: Consumer Finance Purpose: Purchase, Refinance Lien Position: First, Junior Loan Amount: N/A

Term 1 Term 2 Term 3 Term 4 Term 5

Result - #2
  • 2 Versions
  • •
  • 1 Attachment
  • •
  • Version ID: #123

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

Product Type: Mortgages Open / Closed: Closed End, Open End Fixed / Adjustable: Adjustable, Fixed Property Type: Condo, Single Family Max # of Properties: Up to 6 Properties, Multi-Family Occupancy: Primary or Secondary, Non-Owner Occupied Loan Purpose: Consumer Finance Purpose: Purchase, Refinance Lien Position: First, Junior Loan Amount: N/A

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

Product Type: Mortgages Open / Closed: Closed End, Open End Fixed / Adjustable: Adjustable, Fixed Property Type: Condo, Single Family Max # of Properties: Up to 6 Properties, Multi-Family Occupancy: Primary or Secondary, Non-Owner Occupied Loan Purpose: Consumer Finance Purpose: Purchase, Refinance Lien Position: First, Junior Loan Amount: N/A

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

When a person or other entity's business includes engaging in residential real estate related transactions. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

A person or entity must not discriminate against any person in making available a residential real estate related transaction, or in the terms or conditions of the transaction, because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, or national origin. Ala. Code § 24-8-6(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The private individual owner does not own more than 3 single-family houses at any one time;
  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and
  • A bona fide private individual owner does not own an interest in, nor is there owned or reserved on the owner's behalf, under any express or voluntary agreement, title to or a right to all or a portion of the proceeds from the sale or rental of more than 3 single-family houses at any one time.

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • The use in any manner of the sales or rental facilities or the sales or rental services of a real estate broker, agent, or salesperson, or of the facilities or services of a person in the business of selling or renting dwellings, or of an employee or agent of a broker, agent, salesperson, or person; and
  • The publication, posting, or mailing, after notice, of an advertisement or written notice in violation of Code § 24-8-1 to -15 [the Fair Housing Law].

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This exemption does not prohibit the use of attorneys, escrow agents, abstractors, title companies, and other professional assistance as necessary to perfect or transfer title. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Ala. Code § 24-8-7(a).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

  • In the sale of any single-family house by a private individual owner not residing in the house at the time of the sale or who was not the most recent resident of the house before the sale, this exemption will apply only with respect to one sale within a 24-month period; and

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(b).

This provision does not apply to any single-family house sold or rented by an owner when:

Ala. Code § 24-8-7(c).

This provision does not apply to rooms or units in dwellings containing living quarters occupied or intended to be occupied by no more than 4 families living independently of each other, if the owner actually maintains and occupies 1 of the living quarters as his or her residence. Winnow AI

Ask a question. Winnow AI will provide an answer from our comprensive database of state and federal requirements.

Here are some sample questions to get your started:

  • What percent interest rate can I charge for a short-term loan?
  • After I receive a demand from a debtor, can I wait 30 days to release them from their obligation?
  • How many locations can I operate under a license?
